title: TypeError: expected string or bytes-like object, got 'NoneType'
one_liner: Empty body bug report with no repro, version, or stack trace; a second user reports the same but the issue is unactionable without details.

title: NotImplementedError: Provider claude-3-5-sonnet-...does not support chat
one_liner: User omitted the provider prefix; answer already given in comments (use bedrock:claude-... format) so reply confirming and close.

title: Incorrect Placement of Deep Research Option in Frontend Forms
one_liner: Deep Research appears under the sources form instead of the report-type form; minor frontend reorganization to align with ReportType enum.

title: Retrieval of documents from databases and vectorstores like PostgreSQL/pgvector
one_liner: Feature request for official SQL/pgvector document retrieval; vector_store integration partly exists but a dedicated pgvector path is a larger design decision.

title: Llama.cpp Server: Separate LLM and Embedding Endpoints
one_liner: Request to point embeddings at a different OpenAI-compatible base URL than the LLM; add OPENAI_EMBEDDING_BASE_URL support (a contributor flagged a PR; issue 1380 has the concrete patch).

title: Using every documents in local mode
one_liner: Only a fraction of uploaded docs used; likely similarity-threshold/embedding retrieval limiting, needs config guidance (SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD, MAX_SEARCH_RESULTS) or more info.

title: KeyError: 'organic' error while calling the execution via Docker Linux
one_liner: Serper returns an error payload (e.g. 'Query too long. Max 2048 characters') instead of 'organic', causing a KeyError; add graceful handling of Serper error responses.

title: researcher.write_report() yields incomplete/inconsistent results
one_liner: Truncated deep-research reports caused by token limits and provider TPM caps; maintainer already advised raising SMART_TOKEN_LIMIT, resolved—reply and close.

title: Out Of Memory with deep research + local embedding model
one_liner: Local embedding model competes with LLM for VRAM; a contributor proposes adding OPENAI_EMBEDDING_BASE_URL to CustomEmbedding so embeddings can be offloaded to a separate endpoint (overlaps 1367).

title: Markdown tables wrong format and cause write_md_to_pdf to fail
one_liner: Gemini-generated markdown tables are malformed and crash PDF/Word export; needs either prompt hardening or defensive table sanitization in the md-to-doc conversion.

title: Deep research - request too large for gpt-4.1
one_liner: 429 TPM rate-limit from OpenAI on gpt-4.1, not a GPTR bug; maintainer already responded recommending o4-mini and tuning deep-research params—close.

title: Could not connect to MCP Server via HTTP
one_liner: MCP server moved to a dedicated repo (assafelovic/gptr-mcp); maintainer already redirected the user—close as moved.

title: The function get_research_sources has garbled characters
one_liner: Non-ASCII/special characters render as garbled (encoding issue) in research sources; image-only report, needs repro details but likely an encoding fix in source handling.

title: Deep Research - Runtime error (temperature None for ChatOpenAI)
one_liner: Pydantic temperature=None validation error on reasoning models; current base.py has NO_SUPPORT_TEMPERATURE_MODELS handling, so likely already fixed—verify and close.

title: Specifying time range for research
one_liner: Request to expose a time-range filter so searches are constrained to a recency window; needs per-retriever support and an API/config surface decision.

title: Self hosted firecrawl returning 0 scraped results
one_liner: Firecrawl response accessed as dict instead of object attributes; current firecrawl.py already uses dot notation (response.metadata/response.markdown), so likely fixed—verify and close (duplicate of 1423).

title: document_urls path inconsistent with get_research_context() reference path
one_liner: Passed document_urls paths don't match the citation paths generated in research context; image-only report (Chinese), needs repro but plausibly a path-normalization fix.

title: Source verification failed: 'PromptFamily' object has no attribute 'curate_soures'
one_liner: Typo curate_soures vs curate_sources; current code uses the correct spelling everywhere (prompts.py, curator.py), so already fixed—close.

title: Reports with redundant, disorganized content with mis-matching TOC
one_liner: Multi-agent report generator consistently yields redundant content, mismatched TOC, and excessive nesting; a structural prompt/orchestration quality issue requiring maintainer direction.

title: 'ScrapeResponse' object is not subscriptable - even after updating to latest package
one_liner: Firecrawl ScrapeResponse accessed with subscript; current firecrawl.py uses attribute access, so likely resolved on master—verify against a fresh release and close (relates to 1404).

title: choose_agent failing on OpenAI models that don't support temperature
one_liner: o3 fails in choose_agent due to unsupported temperature; base.py now maintains NO_SUPPORT_TEMPERATURE_MODELS including o3, so likely fixed—verify choose_agent path and close.

title: Structured JSON output
one_liner: User asks how to get schema-constrained JSON output; not currently a first-class feature—answer with custom_prompt/post-processing guidance and close (or label as feature if desired).

title: Can't use Groq provider
one_liner: GPTR still demands OPENAI_API_KEY despite Groq config; Groq is supported in base.py but EMBEDDING likely still defaults to OpenAI—needs full config to confirm vs ask reporter.

title: Customize the Report prompt
one_liner: User wants a custom_prompt argument on write_report(); this already exists (write_report(custom_prompt=...)), so reply pointing to it and close.

title: Install Poetry dependencies will cause incompatible constraints
one_liner: pyproject.toml has conflicting json-repair constraints between [tool.poetry] and [project] sections causing poetry install to fail; reconcile the dependency versions.

title: Error using SCRAPER=browser (missing overlay.js)
one_liner: Selenium scraper fails because js/overlay.js isn't shipped in the pip package; add the js data files to package_data/MANIFEST so they install alongside the module.
